LAW AND MOTION 301, HE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: RESPONDENT ROBERT HEDRICK'S MOTION TO DISMISS APPELLANT'S APPEAL DE NOVO FROM AN ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER AND FOR ATTYS FEES AND COSTS; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO POST AN UNDERTAKING AND FOR ATTYS FEES AND COSTS; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING APPELLANT TO POST A BOND WITHIN 10 DAYS, MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. GIVEN THAT SERVICE OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S ORDER WAS ON A DIFFERENT PARTY, PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $251,546.65 WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AS A CONDITION TO PROCEEDING WITH THIS APPEAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-16-2010 IN 206, RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL AND/OR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS GRANTED. ARI HERSHER APPEARING FOR APPELLANT. HILLARY BAKER APPEARING FOR RESPONDENT. THE TRIAL DATE OF 8/30/10 IS CONTINUED TO 3/28/2011 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE COURT ORDERED RESPONDENT TO TAKE THREE DEPOSITIONS FOR A TOTAL OF TWO DAYS AND APPELLANT TO TAKE TWO DEPOSITIONS FOR A TOTAL OF TWO DAYS, WITH THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF TO BE TAKEN FOR ONE DAY. IF PARTIES NEED ADDITIONAL TIME FOR DEPOSITIONS, THEN A NOTICED MOTION WILL BE FILED WITH DEPT. 206. PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED WITH A DISCOVERY PLAN IN WRITING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
JOINT STATEMENT RE: TIME LIMITS FILED BY RESPONDENT HEDRICK JR., ROBERT KENNY

Respondent'S Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Appellant'S Appeal De Novo From An Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner And For Attys Fees And Costs; Or In The Alternative To Dismiss For Failure To Post An Undertaking And For Attys Fees And Costs; Or In The Alternative For An Order Compelling Appellant To Post A Bond Within 10 Days
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRIAN T. ASHE OF THE SEYFARTH & SHAW LLP, TEL. NO. 544-1029 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HILLARY JO BAKER & ATTY. BRYAN SCHWARTZ OF THE BRYAN SCHWARTZ LAW, TEL. NO. (510) 444-9300 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AND MOVING PARTY

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: RESPONDENT ROBERT HEDRICK'S Motion To Dismiss Appellant'S Appeal De Novo From An Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner And For Attys Fees And Costs; Or In The Alternative To Dismiss For Failure To Post An Undertaking And For Attys Fees And Costs; Or In The Alternative For An Order Compelling Appellant To Post A Bond Within 10 Days, MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. GIVEN THAT SERVICE OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S ORDER WAS ON A DIFFERENT PARTY, PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $251,546.65 WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AS A CONDITION TO PROCEEDING WITH THIS APPEAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
